scenario.context: Station 01: UI project kickoff with follow-up assignments from requirements to acceptance preparation.
scenario.mission: You handle the kickoff task and several follow-up tasks for the same interface with growing responsibility.

station.profile: Requirements specification, UI concept, implementation, and testing
constraints: Put user orientation before technical gimmicks
Keep documentation for operations and support in mind

customerRequest.subject: Station 01: UI for a service portal with follow-up tasks
customerRequest.body:
Please start the concept phase for a service-portal interface for ticket intake and status maintenance.

After the kickoff task, additional customer impulses on architecture, framework selection, testing, and documentation will follow.

questions.title: Start gathering requirements
questions.prompt: Which first step makes the most sense for a viable UI plan?

options.text: Implement the final design immediately
options.rationale: Without a requirements picture, missteps are likely.

options.text: Capture user roles, tasks, and the process flow in a structured way
options.rationale: Creates the basis for all follow-up decisions.

options.text: Define only colors and fonts
options.rationale: Visual details do not replace analysis.

feedback.correct: Correct: A clean requirements basis is the starting point.
feedback.wrong: Not suitable: Without a requirements picture, the planning stays unstable.

questions.title: Build the requirements catalog
questions.prompt: Which contents belong in a good UI requirements catalog? (Multiple choice)

options.text: Record user goals and main tasks for each role
Define acceptance criteria for critical functions
Adopt only the visual preferences of individual people
Include non-functional requirements
options.rationale: Performance and accessibility influence the architecture.

options.text: Hide the interfaces to the business logic
options.rationale: UI and business logic have to be aligned.

feedback.correct: Correct: The catalog is professionally and technically robust.
feedback.wrong: Partly wrong: Please combine functional and non-functional aspects more clearly.

questions.title: Structure the design process
questions.prompt: Put the typical flow for an initial UI concept in order.
items: Analyze the as-is process and user problems
Sketch wireframes and the interaction flow
Walk through the prototype with stakeholders
correctOrder: Analyze the as-is process and user problems
Sketch wireframes and the interaction flow
Walk through the prototype with stakeholders

feedback.correct: Correct: The design flow is methodically coherent.
feedback.wrong: Not ideal: Please do analysis and requirements first, then design and documentation.

questions.title: Classify the architecture pattern
questions.prompt: Which statement about UI architecture makes the most sense?

options.text: Put the UI and business logic into one shared class
Separate the presentation layer and the business logic clearly
Define the architecture only after go-live

feedback.correct: Correct: Separating responsibilities is decisive here.
feedback.wrong: Not suitable: The architecture has to be clear early on.

questions.title: Apply ergonomic criteria
questions.prompt: Which criteria speak for a functional and ergonomic UI? (Multiple choice)

options.text: Consistent behavior of recurring elements
Clear feedback for input and errors
Important form elements without labels
Meaningful keyboard operation for frequent actions
Completely different navigation on each screen

feedback.correct: Correct: These criteria support good usability.
feedback.wrong: Partly wrong: Please give more weight to consistency and feedback.

questions.title: Justify GUI elements
questions.prompt: Describe in 4-6 sentences how you would choose suitable GUI elements for ticket intake from the user and process perspective.
questions.expectedOutline: Choice of elements based on role needs, task frequency, and consistent interaction.

feedback.correct: Correct: The choice is justified from the user and process perspective.
feedback.wrong: Please connect the user perspective, task context, and consistency more clearly.

questions.title: Effort for wireframes
questions.prompt: 7 wireframes are created at 35 minutes each. What is the total effort in minutes?

feedback.correct: Calculated correctly.
feedback.wrong: Please multiply the quantity and time per wireframe correctly.

questions.title: Acceptance criteria for the prototype
questions.prompt: Which points make sense for prototype acceptance? (Multiple choice)

options.text: Critical user paths are fully clickable
Field validation is visible for required fields
Only the color choice is evaluated
Feedback for errors is understandable
Open blockers are not documented

feedback.correct: Correct: The criteria secure usability and transparency.
feedback.wrong: Partly wrong: Please include the core flow, validation, and blocker documentation.

questions.title: Secure the framework selection
questions.prompt: Which approach is the most sustainable for choosing a UI framework?

options.text: Choose the framework with the biggest social-media reach
Focus only on short-term implementation speed
Weigh maintainability, team knowledge, LTS, and integration capability

feedback.correct: Correct: That is exactly how the selection becomes professionally robust.
feedback.wrong: Not suitable: Choosing a framework needs more than speed arguments.

questions.title: Follow-up task 1 - Shift the architecture toward a web focus
questions.subject: Desktop share is shrinking, browser usage is rising
questions.body: Please update the UI design for stronger browser usage and check the impact on the framework and component selection.

questions.title: Process the architecture shift
questions.prompt: Put the flow for switching to a web focus in order.
items: Evaluate the architectural impact on the UI components
correctOrder: Evaluate the architectural impact on the UI components

feedback.correct: Correct: The transition is structured in a technically clean way.
feedback.wrong: Not ideal: Please place analysis and realignment before implementation.

questions.title: Make the web UI robust
questions.prompt: Which measures make sense for implementing a web-based UI? (Multiple choice)

options.text: Plan a responsive layout for typical resolutions
Align form validation on the client and server side
Keep error messages technically cryptic
Keep interaction patterns consistent across pages
Ignore accessibility aspects
options.rationale: Not acceptable in a business environment.

feedback.correct: Correct: This makes the web UI robust and user-friendly.
feedback.wrong: Partly wrong: Please secure consistency, validation, and accessibility.

questions.title: Implementation window
questions.prompt: There are 3 days left with 6.5 hours each for the migration. How many hours are available in total?

feedback.correct: Calculated correctly.
feedback.wrong: Please multiply the number of days and hours per day correctly.

questions.title: Assess framework risk
questions.prompt: Which risk needs special attention during a fast framework change?

options.text: Lock-in and a missing maintenance strategy
Too many colorful icons in the mock-up
Users asking questions
options.rationale: A normal part of the process.

feedback.correct: Correct: Lock-in and maintainability are decisive here.
feedback.wrong: Not suitable: The migration needs a clear long-term view.

questions.title: Create a change note
questions.prompt: Write a short change note (4-6 sentences) covering the architectural impact, the decision, and a remaining point.
questions.expectedOutline: Impact, a justified decision, and a clear remaining point for follow-up work.

feedback.correct: Correct: The note is usable for the team and acceptance.
feedback.wrong: Please present the architectural impact, the decision, and the remaining point more clearly.

questions.title: Follow-up task 2 - UX test round with findings
questions.subject: Users report issues during ticket creation
questions.body: The UX round shows breaks in navigation and error messages. Please prioritize the findings and plan follow-up tests.

questions.title: Prioritize UX findings
questions.prompt: Which criteria help prioritize UX findings? (Multiple choice)

options.text: Frequency and impact on the core process
Consequences of the defect for the user's task
Only the team's personal taste
Combine implementation effort and risk
Close findings without a follow-up test

feedback.correct: Correct: The prioritization is professionally sound.
feedback.wrong: Partly wrong: Please consider process impact and risk more strongly.

questions.title: Follow-up test process
questions.prompt: Put the flow from a UX finding to the follow-up test in order.
items: Document the finding with its context
Run the follow-up test with the same scenario
correctOrder: Document the finding with its context
Run the follow-up test with the same scenario

feedback.correct: Correct: This is a clean improvement cycle.
feedback.wrong: Not ideal: Please keep documentation and prioritization before implementation.

questions.title: Finding rate
questions.prompt: Out of 26 UX findings, 18 were fixed in the first round. What is the resolution rate in percent (rounded)?

feedback.correct: Calculated correctly.
feedback.wrong: Please calculate the share of fixed findings correctly.

questions.title: UX status message
questions.prompt: Formulate a UX status message (4-6 sentences) with the finding situation, the prioritization, and the next action.
questions.expectedOutline: Clear finding status, prioritization logic, and a concrete follow-up action.

feedback.correct: Correct: The message is useful for steering and teamwork.
feedback.wrong: Please structure the finding, prioritization, and follow-up action more clearly.

questions.title: Follow-up task 3 - Documentation review
questions.subject: Expand the user documentation for support
questions.body: Support needs clearer documentation for operating flows and error cases. Please sharpen the documentation and version it.

questions.title: Improve the documentation structure
questions.prompt: Which focus is most important for support documentation?

options.text: Structure operating flows, error messages, and solution steps clearly
Describe design decisions only
options.rationale: Not enough for support on its own.

options.text: Do not document versions

feedback.correct: Correct: Support needs exactly these contents.
feedback.wrong: Not suitable: Support documentation needs operating flow, error, and version references.

questions.title: Versioning in the documentation
questions.prompt: Which points belong in clean documentation versioning? (Multiple choice)

options.text: Version number with change date
Short description of the content change
Changes without reference to the affected area
Reference to the responsible team
Delete the history with every change

feedback.correct: Correct: The versioning is built to be maintainable.
feedback.wrong: Partly wrong: Please keep the version and change references clearer.

questions.title: Note the documentation update
questions.prompt: Write a documentation update in 4-6 sentences with the target audience, the change, and an open remaining point.
questions.expectedOutline: Target-audience reference, the concrete change, and a transparent remaining point.

feedback.correct: Correct: The update is suitable for support and the team.
feedback.wrong: Please develop the target audience, the change, and the remaining point more clearly.

questions.title: Follow-up task 4 - Acceptance window today at 16:00
questions.subject: Delivery with testing and documentation evidence
questions.body: Please prepare the final delivery by 16:00: test status, remaining points, and documentation must be clear.

questions.title: Delivery path until 16:00
questions.prompt: Put the flow for a clean UI delivery in order.
items: Record remaining points with a target date
correctOrder: Record remaining points with a target date

feedback.correct: Correct: The delivery is secured in a traceable way.
feedback.wrong: Not ideal: Please secure the test and remaining-point logic before approval.

questions.title: Test coverage before delivery
questions.prompt: Out of 31 planned acceptance tests, 27 were completed successfully. What is the success rate in percent (rounded)?

feedback.correct: Calculated correctly.
feedback.wrong: Please calculate the share of successful tests correctly.

questions.title: Closing message for the UI station
questions.prompt: Formulate a closing message (5-7 sentences) with the status, an open point, a risk, and the next step.
questions.expectedOutline: Clear status, a transparent remaining point with risk, and a binding follow-up action.

feedback.correct: Correct: The closing message is immediately usable for operations and project management.
feedback.wrong: Please develop the status, the open point, and the next step more clearly.
